faults.name: Gain-setting resistor to ground is open
faults.why: If Rg is not connected, the amplifier can collapse toward unity gain instead of the expected non-inverting gain.
faults.verification_test: Power off and check continuity from the inverting input through Rg to ground.
faults.fix_recipe: Reconnect Rg to ground and retest the output/input amplitude ratio.

faults.name: Feedback resistor value is wrong
faults.why: A swapped Rf or Rg changes the expected gain while the circuit still looks qualitatively correct.
faults.verification_test: Power off and measure Rf and Rg directly.
faults.fix_recipe: Replace the incorrect resistor and recompute gain as 1 + Rf/Rg.

faults.name: Input source reference is not common with circuit ground
faults.why: A floating source reference can make the non-inverting input move outside the valid common-mode range.
faults.verification_test: Check continuity between source ground and circuit ground with power off.
faults.fix_recipe: Tie the signal source reference to circuit ground.
